notes: Region outputs are experimental and comparison-oriented.
Crop provenance is provisional unless later stages attach measured geometry.
This manifest mirrors page-level fields where practical so region-vs-page comparisons stay simple.

regions.typeset_text:
ANDREW RITCHIE of the NEW YORK MUSEUM OF MODERN ART ... Today Topolski has perhaps no equal in reportorial draughtsmanship. ... SIR JOHN ROTHENSTEIN, the Director of the TATE GALLERY, London: ... We should be grateful that an artist of such panoramic vision, animated by so robust an interest in the whole life of man as Feliks Topolski, is among us. ... BERNARD SHAW: ... the greatest living draughtsman; perhaps the greatest of all the impressionists in black and white. ... THE TIMES (London): That unique fortnightly modern broadsheet published ... and syndicated round the world, by air subscription from Tokyo to Buenos Aires. ... REALITES (Paris): The rapid call to arms en masse of Topolski's entire Chronicle line forms a unique structure dans son propre talent et dans l'histoire de l'art contemporain: cette Chronicle. ... CARREFOUR (Paris): Chronique formelle qui marquera une date dans son propre talent et dans l'histoire de l'art contemporain: cette Chronicle. ... THE HINDUSTAN TIMES (New Delhi): Topolski is cosmopolitan in outlook and concept, exploring his Roman and Chinese ancestry as he appeals to the imagination with the surreal element of abstraction and contemporary colours. ... GRAPHIS (Zurich): Wherever he goes he is a great traveller. Wherever he finds himself he chooses to meet new influences which are his choice in the sense that he is a world citizen. ... THE IRISH TIMES (Dublin): ... his pictures are commentaries as well as records. HARPER'S BAZAAR (New York): Lisa Bowell's famous Journal, the Chronicle are devoted to trapping the spirit and shape of our time. ... ART DIRECTION (New York): Chronicle obtained him a medal from the New York Art Directors' Club. ... AMERICAN ARTIST (New York): When has there been an artist who has been as well-equipped to be an epic, panoramic graphic chronicler of the entire world? I believe the answer to be: never until now, and the artist is Feliks Topolski. HEADLINE FROM ARTE (Buenos Aires): Topolski 1 x Comedia Humana
CORONATION OF H.M. Queen Elizabeth II. No. 2 Sir Winston Churchill. No. 3 London. No. 4 Conferences and Congresses; No. 6 Spain II—Madrid; No. 7 Spain III; No. 8 England—County Horse Shows; No. 9 London Freaks, Misfits, Circ ... No. 10-11 Mid-Century Retrospect; No. 12 Mother and Child.

regions.page_summary: This crop contains excerpts from reviews and commentary on Topolski's Chronicle, along with a sequential, numbered index of issues and their key thematic subjects and events spanning 1953 to 1956. Multiple languages, editorial testimonials, and issue listings for Topolski's Chronicle are visible, together with several illustrated vignette sketches.

regions.typeset_text: Topolski has perhaps no equal as reportorial draughtsman. – SIR JOHN ROTHENSTEIN the Director of the TATE GALLERY, London: ... We should be grateful that an artist of such panoramic vision, animated by so robust an insight in the whole life of man as Felix Topolski, is at work among us. – BERNARD SHAW ... Topolski...an astonishing draughtsman; perhaps the greatest all the impressionists in black and white. THE TIMES (London): That unique fortnightly modern broadsheet published, written, signed single-handed and able to boast an array of subscribers from Tokyo to Buenos Aires. ... REALITES (Paris): La collection de ce magazine, veritable anthologie de la vie de ce temps, parait destinee a devenir la derniere que le talent de Topolski transforme son chronicle une oeuvre d’art traversera le temps comme celles de ses grands predecesseurs. ... CARREFOUR (Paris): ... presente avec sa Chronicle une formule ormale qu marquera une date dans son propre talent et dans l’histoire de l’art contemporain ... cette Chronicle ... semble repondre et correspondre a une attente encore inexprimee, a une orientation latente du gout moderne du surrealisme et d’abstraction. THE HINDUSTAN TIMES (New Delhi): ... Topolski is cosmopolitan in outlook and consequently appeals to the man in the street wherever he goes. ... GRAPHIS (Zurich): Wherever he goes he is a great traveller, he preserves what he sees for his Chronicle in the way that the camera can never hope to do. IRISH TIMES (Dublin): ... like Boswell’s famous Journal, the Chronicles are devoted to trapping the spirit of our time. ART DIRECTION AWARD: ... The Chronicles earned him a medal from the New York Art Directors Club. ... AMERICAN ARTIST (New York): ...When has there been an artist to blend the daring, energy and skill to entwine allied form and matter on such a scale as this? HEADLINE FROM ARTE (Buenos Aires): ... ‘Topolski y la Comedia Humana.’
